With their iconic Predator details and timeless design, they're a statement of your passion for the game.

about.title: CLASSIC NYLON
about.description: Iconic low profile trainers with rubber outsole for grip.A true footwear icon, the Samba OG Shoes need little introduction. Born from football culture, these adidas trainers quickly moved from terraces to city streets and dancefloors. New colourways and materials have evolved the silhouette, bringing fresh style while staying true to the original style. The iconic leather upper, serrated 3-Stripes, and Samba name on the lateral side create a timeless look that embodies the adidas Originals spirit. The synthetic lining provides a snug fit, while the rubber outsole delivers grip and durability. A lace-up closure keeps feet secure, combining practicality with style for everyday wear. Whether exploring city streets or enjoying a casual day out, these shoes work as well with skirts and dresses as they do with jeans or sportswear, carrying the Samba’s heritage forward with modern touches that give a fresh edge to a classic silhouette. Step into OG Samba style and celebrate the enduring spirit of adidas Originals.

about.title: CLASSIC NYLON
about.description: ASICS SportStyle announces the second collaboration with Toyoda Gosei. Featuring the SKYHAND™ OG sneaker, this collaboration explores the topic of becoming more sustainable by reusing Toyoda Gosei’s leftover car leather material that was used for their steering wheels. ASICS and Toyoda Gosei are both committed to becoming more sustainable brands. Therefore, the design teams found a way to repurpose the discarded content from Toyoda Gosei’s steering wheel manufacturing process as a unique design element for the SKYHAND™ OG sneaker. Designed with simplicity and everyday wearability in mind, the SKYHAND™ OG sneaker features a primarily tonal black color palette. Various leathers with suede, perforated, and smooth textures create a monochromatic patchwork effect throughout the upper. Additionally, a prominent cross-stitching connects the quarter-panel and heel as a nod to the seams that are prevalent on the auto manufacturer’s steering wheel designs.

details.Address: Dlf Horizon Centre - 2, 6Th Floor, Sector - 43,Dlf Phase - 5, Golf Course Road, Gurugram

about.title: CLASSIC NYLON
about.description: ASICS SportStyle announces the second collaboration with Toyoda Gosei. Featuring the SKYHAND™ OG sneaker, this collaboration explores the topic of becoming more sustainable by reusing Toyoda Gosei’s leftover car leather material that was used for their steering wheels. ASICS and Toyoda Gosei are both committed to becoming more sustainable brands. Therefore, the design teams found a way to repurpose the discarded content from Toyoda Gosei’s steering wheel manufacturing process as a unique design element for the SKYHAND™ OG sneaker. Designed with simplicity and everyday wearability in mind, the SKYHAND™ OG sneaker features a primarily tonal black color palette. Various leathers with suede, perforated, and smooth textures create a monochromatic patchwork effect throughout the upper. Additionally, a prominent cross-stitching connects the quarter-panel and heel as a nod to the seams that are prevalent on the auto manufacturer’s steering wheel designs.

details.Address: Dlf Horizon Centre - 2, 6Th Floor, Sector - 43,Dlf Phase - 5, Golf Course Road, Gurugram

about.title: CLASSIC NYLON
about.description: The original MR530 combined turn of the millennium aesthetics with the reliability of a high milage running shoe. The reintroduced 530 applies a contemporary, everyday style outlook to this performance-minded design. A segmented ABZORB midsole is paired with a classic mesh and synthetic overlay upper design, which utilizes sweeping curves and angles for a distinctive, high-tech look.

about.title: CLASSIC NYLON
about.description: The 1906R, like its cousins the 2002R and the 860v2, is led by a sole unit featuring a combination of flexible ACTEVA LITE cushioning, shock absorbing N-ergy, and segmented ABZORB SBS pods at the heel. This hi-tech approach is also reflected in the 1906R’s upper design, which features open-holed mesh and a series of curvilinear synthetic overlays. This distinctive take on the era’s design conventions offers a refined execution of high-performance heritage.

about.title: CLASSIC NYLON
about.description: The most New Balance shoe ever’ says it all, right? No, actually. The 574 might be our unlikeliest icon. The 574 was built to be a reliable shoe that could do a lot of different things well rather than as a platform for revolutionary technology, or as a premium materials showcase. This unassuming, unpretentious versatility is exactly what launched the 574 into the ranks of all-time greats. As hybrid road/trail design built on a wider last than the previous generation’s narrow racing silhouettes, the 574 offered a uniquely versatile mix of new, different, uncomplicated, rugged, durable, and comfortable that was adopted as a closet staple across the globe. That’s why today, the 574 is synonymous with the boundary defying New Balance style, and worn by anyone.

about.title: CLASSIC NYLON
about.description: As recreational running took off in the 1970s, the benchmark for athletic footwear shifted from mere existence to performance. While the designs of the era might be considered basic by today's standards, that decade stands out as the golden age of running shoes. The 327 sheds new light on the '70s as a time of innovation, boldly reimagining classic design elements with a thoroughly contemporary perspective. Featuring an angular reworking of the tried-and-tested wedge silhouette, an oversized, asymmetrically applied "N" logo, and a wraparound outsole with trail-inspired lugs, the 327 offers nothing less than a complete reinvention of our running heritage.

about.title: CLASSIC NYLON
about.description: New Balance & Salehe Bembury come together once again, bringing Salehe's nature-inspired vision to the classic 1000 sneaker. With 'Fog be the cloud,' the turn-of-the-millennium classic is infused with rich textures and colors drawn from the Smoky Mountains. Mirroring the lush landscape's diversity, Bembury combines leather overlays with hairy suede tips, spacer mesh vamps, and rubber outsoles. The shoe's calming blue comes from the mountain range's iconic ridges, and is fused with the sunset's coral pink, and the deep tones of the forest below. The resulting design is as distinctive as the tranquil terrain it reflects.

about.title: CLASSIC NYLON
about.description: The original 550 debuted in 1989 and made its mark on basketball courts from coast to coast. After its initial run, the 550 was filed away in the archives, before being reintroduced in limited-edition releases in late 2020, and returned to the full-time lineup in 2021, quickly becoming a global fashion favorite. The 550’s low top, streamlined silhouette offers a clean take on the heavy-duty designs of the late ‘80s, while the dependable leather, synthetic, and mesh upper construction is a classic look in any era.

about.title: CLASSIC NYLON
about.description: ‘The most New Balance shoe ever’ says it all, right? No, actually. The 574 might be our unlikeliest icon. The 574 was built to be a reliable shoe that could do a lot of different things well rather than as a platform for revolutionary technology, or as a premium materials showcase. This unassuming, unpretentious versatility is exactly what launched the 574 into the ranks of all-time greats. As hybrid road/trail design built on a wider last than the previous generation’s narrow racing silhouettes, the 574 offered a uniquely versatile mix of new, different, uncomplicated, rugged, durable, and comfortable that was adopted as a closet staple across the globe. That’s why today, the 574 is synonymous with the boundary defying New Balance style, and worn by anyone.

about.title: CLASSIC NYLON
about.description: With design language pulled from a track spike from 1961 – the R400 has DNA rooted in one of the first ever Reebok shoes. The original model featured unique dual branding, as it was created in a transitional period where the Foster brothers re-branded their growing athletic footwear company ’Mercury Sports’ to ‘Reebok’ in 1960. The unique dual-branding is in reference to the lateral torch-shaped gesture on the original 1961 model, in reference to the Greek God Mercury and the old Mercury Sports company name.
